commit 34395954d4e881c864e04e000d0e54ffa24cf107
Author: Fabien Penso <gpg@pen.so>
Date:   Wed Apr 1 20:30:36 2026 +0100

    fix(web): preserve server error message for model probes
    
    localizeRpcError was replacing the server's specific error (e.g. "You
    do not have permission to access glm-5.1") with a generic locale
    string ("Service temporarily unavailable") because all non-Forbidden
    ServiceErrors map to the UNAVAILABLE error code. Preserve the original
    server message as serverMessage so testModel can show the actual
    backend reason.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: ea077d79d02a

Fix applied. Summary of findings and fix:

**Root cause**: All three CI failures are the same E2E test (`chat-input.spec.js:225` — "chat.full_context reports workspace prompt truncation"). The second run also had a flaky `settings-nav` test that passed on retry.

The test's `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse` helper expected `error.message` to contain "no LLM providers configured" so it could skip when CI has no providers configured. But commit `34395954` ("fix(web): preserve server error message for model probes") changed `localizeRpcError` in `helpers.js` to replace `error.message` with a locale string ("An internal server error occurred.") and move the original backend message to `serverMessage`. The test was never updated, so it stopped detecting the no-providers case and failed on `expect(fullContextRpc?.ok).toBe(true)`.

**Fix**: Check both `serverMessage` and `message` in `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse`.

Want me to commit and push this fix?

content.text:
SSH needs a YubiKey tap. Please tap your YubiKey so I can retry the push.

To your question: yes, exactly. Here's what happened:

- Before commit `34395954` (Apr 1, "preserve server error message for model probes"), the RPC error sent to JS had `error.message = "no LLM providers configured"` directly from the server.
- That commit changed `localizeRpcError` in `crates/web/src/assets/js/helpers.js:243` so that whenever an error has a known code (like `INTERNAL`), it replaces `error.message` with a generic localized string (`"An internal server error occurred."` from `locales/en/errors.js`) and moves the original backend message to a new field, `error.serverMessage`.
- The server still maps "no LLM providers configured" to `ServiceError::Message` → `ErrorShape` with code `INTERNAL` (see `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs:47`), so after localization the client sees:
  - `error.code = "INTERNAL"`
  - `error.message = "An internal server error occurred."` (generic)
  - `error.serverMessage = "no LLM providers configured"` (the real one)
- The E2E helper `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse` only looked at `error.message`, so it stopped recognizing the no-providers case and the test proceeded to assert `ok === true`, which failed.

The fix checks `serverMessage` first, falling back to `message`. An alternative deeper fix would be to map "no LLM providers configured" to the `UNAVAILABLE` code on the server side instead of `INTERNAL`, but that's a broader semantic change — the helper fix is localized and keeps the existing error taxonomy intact.

Let me know once the YubiKey is tapped and I'll retry the push.
